zh.Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England: 英國諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯
zh.of Northamptonshire, England: 英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Eudes la Zouche於1297年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯時，他的父親William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche21歲，母親Maud Lovell18歲。他於大約1313年與Joan Inge在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1326年4月27日在法國瑟芭黎絲享年29歲，埋葬在法國瑟芭黎絲。

zh-hans.Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England: 英国諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯
zh-hans.of Northamptonshire, England: 英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eudes la Zouche于1297年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯时，他的父亲William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche21岁，母亲Maud Lovell18岁。他于大约1313年与Joan Inge在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1326年4月27日在法国瑟芭黎絲享年29岁，埋葬在法国瑟芭黎絲。

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Zouche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse, Chantal, Dominique, Francois, Herve, Michel, Rodolphe, Serge.
French (Fouché): variant of Foucher . Compare Fooshee , Foshee , Fouch , and Foushee .
Altered form (Fouché) of French Foucher (compare 1 above), found mainly in South Africa.

variants.name: Foucher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Etienne, Henri, Lydie, Marcel, Rosaire.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Folchari, composed of the elements folk, folc ‘people’ and hari ‘army’. Compare Faucher 2.

variants.name: Foushee
meanings: Americanized form of French Fouché (see Fouche 1).

History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its original form Fouché) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Fooshee
meanings: Americanized form of French Fouché (see Fouche 1).

variants.name: Foshee
meanings: Americanized form of French Fouché (see Fouche 1).

variants.name: Fouch
meanings: Altered form of French Fouché (see Fouche 1).
Altered form of   Fouts  , a surname of German origin.
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Fudge  .

nameMeanings.name: Eudes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Eade , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Eads
meanings: and Norwegian equivalent of Matthew .

variants.name: Eade
meanings: Respelling of   Cade .

variants.name: Edes
meanings: This name is found as a variant or pet form of Edith in Yorkshire from the 15th century onwards. It is recorded as a baptismal name in the 16th century. In present-day use it probably refers to the biblical ‘Garden of Eden’, crew ῾eden ‘place of pleasure’. As a boy's name it may also represent a variant of Aidan or a transferred use of a surname, itself derived in the Middle Ages from a given name Edun or Edon. This is of Old English origin, from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + hūn ‘bear cub’.

variants.name: Edds
meanings: Pet form of any of several old German names beginning with Ed-. The Elder and Younger Eddas are also the names of two influential Old Icelandic books of Norse myths and poems.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Eudes la Zouche was born in 1297, in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, his father, William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche, was 21 and his mother, Maud Lovell, was 18. He married Joan Inge about 1313, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. He died on 27 April 1326, in Paris, Seine, France, at the age of 29, and was buried in Paris, Seine, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eudes la Zouche est né en 1297, à Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Maud Lovell, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Joan Inge environ 1313, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 27 avril 1326, à Paris, Seine, France, à l’âge de 29 ans, et a été enterré à Paris, Seine, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eudes la Zouche 1297 in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche 21 und seine Mutter Maud Lovell 18. Er hat um 1313 in England, United Kingdom Joan Inge geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 27. April 1326 im Alter von 29 Jahren in Paris, Seine, Frankreich und wurde in Paris, Seine, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eudes la Zouche è nato il nel 1297 nel Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, il suo padre, William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Maud Lovell, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Joan Inge circa 1313, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 4 figlie. Morì il 27 aprile 1326, nel Paris, Seine, Francia, all'età di 29 anni, e fu sepolto a Paris, Seine, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Eudes la Zoucheが1297年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアハーリングウオルスで生まれた時，父William la Zouche 1st Baron Zoucheは21歳，母Maud Lovellは18歳であった。約1313年にイギリスイギリスでJoan Ingeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1326年4月27日にフランスセアインパーイスで29歳で死去し，フランスセアインパーイスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Eudes la Zouche(이)가 1297년 영국 노르섬프토실르 해링워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud Lovell(은)는 18세였다. 그는 대략 1313년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Joan Inge(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1326년 4월 27일 프랑스 세이인 파리스에서 29세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 세이인 파리스에 묻혔다.

mn.of Northamptonshire, England: North Hamtunscire, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eudes la Zouche nasceu em 1297, em Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Maud Lovell, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Inge aproximadamente 1313, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 27 de abril de 1326, em Paris, Seine, França, com 29 anos, e foi sepultado em Paris, Seine, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eudes la Zouche родился в 1297 г. в местности харрингворт, нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Maud Lovell, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Inge около 1313 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Он умер 27 апреля 1326 г. в местности пэрис, син, Франция в возрасте 29 лет и был похоронен в местности пэрис, син, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Eudes la Zouche nació en 1297, en Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Maud Lovell, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Joan Inge aproximadamente 1313, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 4 hijas. Murió el 27 de abril de 1326, en Paris, Seine, Francia, a la edad de 29 años, y fue sepultado en Paris, Seine, Francia.
